seoTitle: Tilligerry Creek vs Timbarra — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Tilligerry Creek and Timbarra. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Tilligerry Creek vs Timbarra: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tilligerry Creek and Timbarra.
heading: Tilligerry Creek vs Timbarra — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tilligerry Creek, New South Wales and Timbarra, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Timbarra has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Tilligerry Creek (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tilligerry Creek or Timbarra safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tilligerry Creek has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Timbarra scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tilligerry Creek scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Timbarra scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tilligerry Creek or Timbarra?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Timbarra has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
